checks.a_analysis: The output document follows the source PDF closely. The AI has added some decorative metadata like table captions ('Role Definitions') and summaries, which are explicitly allowed under the 'ACCEPTABLE CHANGES' criteria. No fabricated body text or invented policy requirements were found.
The source document does not contain specific contact information such as phone numbers or email addresses, and the output correctly reflects this. No URLs were fabricated.

While all primary section headers (PE-1 through PE-20 and Enforcement) are present, a significant portion of Page 12 is missing. However, because the headers for the sections on that page exist, this is categorized as a significant omission within a section rather than a missing page or section.

The document contains one primary table under 'Responsibilities'. The source PDF table has 4 data rows (Senior Management, Security Liaison, Facility Management, Third Parties). The output table faithfully reproduces all 4 rows with their respective definitions.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: In section PE-17 (Alternate Work Site), the output document omits a significant portion of the requirements that appear at the top of Page 12 of the source. Specifically, nested list items d.ii through d.vi and top-level list item e are entirely absent. These items cover critical security requirements for secure data storage at alternate sites, equipment security at home/hotel sites, and vehicle transport rules. This omission represents more than 25% of the content for this specific section.
checks.b_evidence_source: ii. Design requirements for secure data storage (e.g., fire suppression and detection equipment, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning [HVAC], measures to prevent water damage, etc.). iii. Equipment being used or stored at an individual alternate work site, such as hotel, home, or other alternate site, must be secured when not in use. iv. Equipment transported in vehicles must be hidden from casual view. v. Equipment must not be stored in vehicles overnight. vi. NIST SP 800-46, Revision 2 must be used as guidance for security in telework and remote access. e. This control is optional for LOW risk information systems.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list for PE-17 (element index 48) ends abruptly after item d.i: 'i. Sufficient physical access controls (e.g., doors, locks, etc.) to the site and to the agency’s data store.'

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Scanning the preserved text, the technical details such as policy IDs (SCIO-SEC-313), dates (01/29/2018), and specific numbers (e.g., UPS battery limit of 'four (4) hours') are accurately transcribed. Minor whitespace and list formatting differences are consistent with acceptable OCR/reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: I examined the text blocks and images in the output JSON. The Great Seal image appears 12 times, which corresponds to its appearance in the header of each of the 12 pages in the source PDF. This is intentional repetition and not a defect. No significant text blocks were found to be duplicated in the output elements.

The heading hierarchy is severely flattened and contains skips. The document title is rendered as an H3 (element index 1), jumping directly from the root level to H3 without H1 or H2. Furthermore, subsection headers like 'PE-6 (1)' (element index 31) and 'PE-13 (1)' (element index 42) are rendered as H3, making them peers to their parent section headers ('PE-6' at index 27 and 'PE-13' at index 40), which are also H3. This results in incorrect nesting and loss of semantic structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: PE-6 (Page 6) is a section header; PE-6 (1) (Page 7) is a subsection. PE-13 (Page 10) is a section; PE-13 (1) (Page 10) is a subsection.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 27 (PE-6) is H3; Element index 31 (PE-6 (1)) is H3. Element index 40 (PE-13) is H3; Element index 42 (PE-13 (1)) is H3.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections PE-6 and PE-13, pages 6, 7, and 10.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains one primary table ('Role Definitions') that spans pages 1 and 2. The pipeline correctly extracted the table and merged the row from the second page ('Third Parties') into the single table structure at element index 7. Headers and columns are correctly identified.

The reading order of the extracted elements matches the logical flow of the source document. Headers, paragraphs, and lists follow the top-to-bottom, page-by-page sequence of the PDF. The metadata fields from the page 1 header box are omitted from the main elements, which is standard for header extraction, but the rest of the content is in the correct order.

Most lists are correctly converted with proper nesting. However, there is a structural failure at the page break between pages 11 and 12. In section PE-17, list item 'd' contains sub-items 'i' through 'vi'. Sub-item 'i' appears on page 11, while 'ii' through 'vi' appear on page 12. In the output JSON (element index 57), item 'd' only contains sub-item 'i'. Sub-items 'ii' through 'vi' are missing, resulting in an incorrectly converted and incomplete list structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: PE-17 (Pages 11-12) list item 'd' contains sub-items 'i' through 'vi'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 57 (PE-17 list) item 'd' only contains nested sub-item 'i'. Items 'ii' through 'vi' are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: PE-17 list, pages 11 and 12, element index 57.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain active hyperlinks. No links were injected or missed in the output JSON. Mentions of other policies (e.g., SCIO-SEC-308) are plain text in the source and remain as plain text in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The hierarchical structure of headings (Scope, Responsibilities, PE-1 through PE-20, Enforcement) is maintained in the same order. The responsive single-column layout is an appropriate conversion from the fixed PDF layout. The repetition of the document header (seal and metadata) corresponds to its appearance on every page of the original 12-page PDF, serving as a clear visual marker for page boundaries in the continuous HTML view.

The 'Role Definitions' table from page 1 of the PDF is correctly rendered in the HTML under the 'Responsibilities' section. It maintains clear columns for 'Role' and 'Definition', uses distinct headers, and includes all rows from the source document (Senior Management, Security Liaison, Facility Management, Third Parties). The table is easily readable and its structure is intact.

The only meaningful image in the source PDF is the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina within the page header. This image is present and correctly rendered throughout the HTML output, appearing at the start of each section corresponding to a new page in the source PDF.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content (paragraphs, list items, or tables). The document metadata from the headers (Document No, Dates, etc.) and the state seal are repeated, but this accurately reflects their presence on every page of the 12-page source PDF. The headings in the 'Contents' navigation are expected design elements and do not constitute duplication of the body headings.
